subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear organization with a comprehensive table of contents, section headings and labelled figures
weaknesses: Coherence is uneven, with abrupt transitions and partly disconnected justifications
Excessive raw data tables and repeated screenshots reduce conciseness
Mixed citation styles in the bibliography detract from consistency
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation strands received band scores of 2 for coherence and conciseness and 3 for organization. While the structure is clear, explanations sometimes jump and there is unnecessary bulk. These factors justify a final score of 2, reflecting some coherence and adequate organization but only mostly relevant content.

strengths: Effective use of multiple representations (tables, diagrams, images, graphs) to support the mathematics
Basic correct use of notation for functions, derivatives and probabilities
weaknesses: Mathematical notation is inconsistent (missing units, unlabeled axes) and sometimes fragmented
Explanations of rules and calculations contain minor algebraic slips and lack full clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical Communication strands were band 2 for language and clarity, and band 3 for representations. The work shows some appropriate notation and effective visuals but suffers from inconsistencies and fragmented explanations, warranting a score of 2.

strengths: Strong personal context through choice of a sports investigation and original data collection
Evidence of independent thinking by devising a âspeed parameterâ and selecting a nonâstandard rational model
weaknesses: Creativity and initiative are good but methods largely adapt existing tools rather than introducing novel techniques
Personal approach is clear but not highly innovative or exceptional
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three Personal Engagement strands were placed in band 2, showing significant independent thinking and a personal approach but not at an outstanding level. A final score of 2 appropriately reflects this substantial yet moderate engagement.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on experimental design limitations and personal biases
Thoughtful analysis of results with clear practical training suggestions
weaknesses: Depth of critical reflection is limited; could prioritise factors and propose mitigation strategies
Evaluation of outcomes lacks comprehensive future implications and deeper theoretical context
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection strands were band 2 across depth, analysis and evaluation. The student demonstrates consistent, meaningful reflection but stops short of substantial critical depth and comprehensive evaluation, justifying a score of 2.

strengths: Relevant SL-level mathematics (probability, rational modelling, calculus) applied appropriately throughout
Calculus and modelling are commensurate with course expectations and support the investigation
weaknesses: Algebraic transcription errors and imprecise interpretations (e.g. speed parameter vs. actual velocity) reduce accuracy
Key methodological details (calibration, goodness-of-fit metrics, statistical justification) are omitted
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Use of Mathematics strands scored band 3 for relevance and level appropriateness but band 2 for understanding and accuracy. The work applies suitable SL mathematics effectively but has errors and missing rigor, resulting in a balanced score of 3.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations of the quotient rule and probability calculations are present but sometimes fragmented and contain minor algebraic slips, giving only partial clarity.  Thus the work demonstrates âsome logical structure and clarity,â not the consistency required for higher bands.

Derivatives are calculated, but algebraic transcription errors and an imprecise interpretation of speed indicate partial understanding with some inaccuracies.  Hence âsome understanding with errorsâ (band 2) is appropriate.

Tables, court diagrams, Logger Pro image sequences and Desmos graphs support the mathematics effectively.  Although not always fully annotated, the variety and generally correct placement of representations fulfils the âeffective useâ descriptor.

The report follows a recognisable introductionâmethodâanalysisâreflection sequence, yet explanations often jump and some justifications (e.g. choice of 20 serves, use of âspeed parameterâ) are only partly connected to later mathematics.  Overall flow is understandable but not consistently logical, indicating âsome coherenceâ rather than the sustained flow expected for higher bands.

Collecting original video data, applying Logger Pro and devising a âspeed parameterâ illustrate good creativity and initiative, though methods largely adapt existing tools rather than introducing novel mathematical techniques.

Results are interpreted in terms of biomechanics and personal strengths; limitations such as shuttle type and camera perspective are acknowledged, showing thoughtfulâthough not deeply theoreticalâanalysis.
The student evaluates practical applicability, outlines specific training actions and suggests future improvements, indicating a clear (but not comprehensive) evaluation of outcomes and implications.

The student reflects on serving preferences, equipment choice and experimental design with several meaningful observations woven through the discussion, matching the descriptor of âmeaningful reflection throughoutâ.

Basic notation for functions, derivatives and probabilities is used, but symbols are sometimes missing (units, vector arrows) and formatting is inconsistent.  Hence âsome appropriate mathematical languageâ fits best.

The investigation is clearly grounded in the studentâs sporting experience; choices of variables, distances and success criteria reflect a personal approach, but innovation remains moderate rather than outstanding.

Most content is relevant; however lengthy raw data tables, repeated screenshots and narrative digressions on personal preference add bulk without mathematical value.  This places the work at âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elements,â not the fully concise level.

Probability, rational-function modelling and differential calculus are relevant to the research question and applied appropriately to arrive at conclusions, satisfying the ârelevant mathematics used appropriatelyâ band.
Designing and carrying out 480 serves, devising personal notation and selecting a non-standard rational function model show significant independent thinking beyond textbook examples, meriting band 2.

A clear table of contents, section headings, labelled figures and sequential numbering show good organisation.  Reader can locate data, modelling, calculus and reflection sections with ease, matching the descriptor of a âwell-organised exploration with clear structure.â

The mathematics (polynomial/rational functions and derivatives) is solidly within SL syllabus expectations and used in context; complexity is moderate, so band 3 (commensurate with course level) is most fitting.

annotations.comment: The research question is stated but could be more precise by defining success criteria and expected outcomes. Clarify the hypothesis and variables to guide the investigation more effectively.
The table of contents is comprehensive but overly detailed. Consider collapsing subsections or using indentation to improve navigability and conciseness.
The introduction effectively shows personal engagement but lacks a concise summary of methodology and mathematical approach. Add a brief outline of techniques and models used.
The rationale for choosing 20 serves is pragmatic but lacks statistical justification. Consider discussing confidence intervals or margin of error to support sample size.
The notation table for serve codes is helpful, but the abbreviations RLD, RRD, etc. should be accompanied by a clear legend or example to avoid reader confusion.
Figure 2 lacks axis labels and scale, making it difficult to interpret distances. Add units and orientation markers to enhance clarity.

The description of probability calculation mentions P(S) but does not provide the formula explicitly. Include $$P(S)=\frac{\text{successful serves}}{\text{total serves}}$$ to strengthen mathematical communication.
The raw data table contains excessive detail. Summarising results with counts or percentages in a concise table would improve relevance and conciseness.
Optimal Distance Results table presents key probabilities but does not indicate sample size. Add a note that each P(S) is based on 20 serves for transparency.
The interpretation in the analysis mixes personal insight with quantitative data but does not reference specific probabilities. Link statements directly to P(S) values to improve logical clarity.
The Logger Pro model description omits calibration details such as scale factor or reference object. Describe how pixels were converted to meters to demonstrate rigor.
The tabulated (X,Y) points lack axis headings and units. Label the columns with units (m) and specify coordinate origin to enhance mathematical communication.
The choice of a rational function is justified by visual fit, but goodness-of-fit metrics (e.g., $$R^2$$ values) are not reported. Include these to support the model selection.
Definitions of parameters a, b, c, etc. are generic. Tailor the descriptions to this modelâs context (e.g., explain how g represents asymptote at impact).
Selecting the derivative point based on video scale and average height introduces potential scaling error. Discuss alternative objective criteria (e.g., maximum speed).
The derivative is calculated correctly using the quotient rule, but the final formula lacks clear simplification steps. Show intermediary algebra to enhance clarity.
The concept of measuring a âspeed parameterâ via derivative is innovative, but it conflates displacement per frame with actual velocity. Acknowledge this distinction explicitly.
The table summarising derivative results lists negative values without taking absolute value. Clarify why the sign is retained and how it relates to speed magnitude.
Reflection acknowledges equipment and perspective limitations but could deepen by prioritizing the most influential factors and proposing mitigation strategies.
The conclusion restates findings but could more explicitly link back to the research question and hypotheses to demonstrate coherence.
The bibliography follows a mixed citation style. Standardize formatting (e.g., APA or MLA) and include all necessary details consistently.
Personal engagement is evident through the choice of a sports context and original data collection. The personal approach demonstrates significant initiative.
The student shows independent thinking by adapting a rational model rather than a standard quadratic. This indicates creativity in selecting an appropriate function.
The reflection on personal confidence and serve preference adds meaningful depth, showing the studentâs awareness of biases in experimental design.
Mathematical notation is mostly consistent but sometimes omits units (e.g., meters for X and Y). Ensure units are always indicated alongside variables.
The logical progression from data collection to modelling to analysis is clear, though some transitions are abrupt. Add brief summaries between sections.
Understanding of calculus concepts is evident, but algebraic slips in the derivative expansion should be rechecked for accuracy.
The use of multiple representations (tables, figures, models) supports the analysis effectively. These visual aids enhance understanding of serve trajectories.
Explicit evaluation of outcomes includes practical training suggestions. This shows clear evaluation, though future implications could be expanded.
The mathematical depth aligns with SL expectations, applying calculus and modelling appropriately. Further theoretical context on shuttle aerodynamics could elevate the analysis.
